title: BOOK5 | Portfolio Management and Ethical and Professional Standards

readings.title: Economics and Investment Markets
readings.sourceNote: 2026 Schweser Book5 backbone + Marie note ledger N0470~N0475, independently rewritten for study use.
readings.overview: Macro shocks가 expected cash flows, default-free term structure, risk premiums을 통해 valuation으로 전달되는 구조와 business-cycle effects를 연결한다.
readings.examPriority: 높음. Expectations surprise, real rates, curve shape, BEI, credit spreads, consumption hedging, multiples와 commercial RE를 valuation channel로 묶어야 한다.

losItems.label: LOS 34.a
losItems.title: How economic factors reach market values
losItems.losText: 경제변수가 시장가치를 바꾸려면 결국 default-free term structure, expected cash-flow timing/amount, 또는 risk premium 중 하나를 움직여야 한다는 valuation channel을 설명해.
losItems.why: 거시지표를 외우는 Reading이 아니라 “macro shock가 valuation equation의 어디로 들어오는가”를 묻는 출발점이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: 경제변수가 시장가치를 바꾸려면 결국 default-free term structure, expected cash-flow timing/amount, 또는 risk premium 중 하나를 움직여야 한다는 valuation channel을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: GDP, inflation, policy, regulation 같은 뉴스가 asset price를 직접 움직이는 게 아니야. 그 뉴스가 future CF를 바꾸거나, risk-free discount rates를 바꾸거나, investors가 요구하는 risk premium을 바꿀 때 price가 움직여.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Asset value는 expected CF의 PV다. Discount rate는 real default-free rate, expected inflation, inflation uncertainty/term components, asset-specific risk premium으로 분해해 생각할 수 있어. 따라서 같은 macro shock도 asset class와 maturity에 따라 전달경로가 다르다.

sections.title: 4. 계산·판단 workflow
bullets: 세 channel의 net effect로 valuation 방향 판단

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0470~N0471은 growth·inflation·policy를 바로 price와 연결하지 않고 expected CF / discount rate / premium의 세 통로로 분해했어. 사이트도 이 transmission map을 기준으로 작성했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Risk-free rate와 risk premium을 같은 변수로 취급
Industry-specific CF shock를 market-wide discount-rate shock로 오해

formulas: V0 = Σ E(CF_t)/(1+k_t)^t
Risk-adjusted discount rate ≈ real default-free rate + expected inflation + relevant risk premiums

quiz.question: Company-specific bad news로 주가만 하락하고 industry index는 거의 unchanged다. 가장 자연스러운 channel은?
quiz.explanation: Industry-wide discount-rate shock보다 company-specific CF/premium 변화가 자연스러워.

quiz.question: 경제변수가 asset value를 바꾸는 직접 valuation channel이 아닌 것은?
quiz.explanation: Valuation에 들어오는 경제적 channel은 CF, risk-free curve, risk premium이 핵심이야.

losItems.label: LOS 34.b
losItems.title: Expectations, surprises and market valuation
losItems.losText: 시장가격은 현재 기대를 이미 반영하므로, 새로운 정보 자체보다 “기존 기대 대비 얼마나 달랐는가”가 valuation을 바꾼다는 점을 설명해.
losItems.why: 실적 20% 성장인데 주가가 하락하는 이유를 이해하는 LOS야. 좋은 숫자와 positive surprise는 같은 말이 아니야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: 시장가격은 현재 기대를 이미 반영하므로, 새로운 정보 자체보다 “기존 기대 대비 얼마나 달랐는가”가 valuation을 바꾼다는 점을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Price는 expected future CF와 discount rate에 대한 consensus를 반영해. 발표치가 좋아도 expectation이 더 높았다면 negative surprise가 되고 price가 하락할 수 있어.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: New information은 expected CF path, terminal assumptions, policy path, risk premium을 수정한다. Market reaction은 level보다 revision size와 persistence에 민감하다.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0470~N0471의 핵심 메모는 “markets price expectations, not today’s data alone”였어. Earnings surprise, policy surprise, inflation surprise를 같은 구조로 묶었어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: One-off surprise를 permanent growth change로 처리

formulas: Price change is driven by changes in expectations, not the absolute level alone

quiz.question: EPS growth 12% 발표 후 주가 하락. 가장 자연스러운 설명은?
Market valuation에서 new information의 핵심은?
quiz.explanation: 현재 가격에는 이미 기대가 반영돼 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 34.c
losItems.title: Growth, growth volatility and real short rates
losItems.losText: 장기 경제성장률과 성장 변동성이 평균 real short-term interest rate와 어떤 관계를 갖는지 consumption/utility 관점에서 설명해.
losItems.why: Real rate를 단순 central-bank number가 아니라 current vs future consumption trade-off로 이해하는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: 장기 경제성장률과 성장 변동성이 평균 real short-term interest rate와 어떤 관계를 갖는지 consumption/utility 관점에서 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Future income/growth가 높을 것으로 예상되면 future consumption의 marginal utility가 낮아지고 사람들은 current consumption을 더 선호해 saving supply가 줄 수 있어. 이를 보상하려면 real rate가 높아지는 방향이 자연스럽다.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Intertemporal marginal rate of substitution은 current consumption을 포기하고 future consumption을 얻는 교환가치를 나타내. Expected growth가 높고 growth uncertainty가 크면 average real rates와 required compensation이 높아질 수 있다.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0471은 consumption, wealth, saving/investment와 business cycle을 연결했어. 이 LOS에서는 그 노트를 intertemporal substitution 직관으로 정리했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Higher growth → lower real rates로 자동 반전
Individual utility story를 exact point forecast로 사용

formulas: Real short rate is linked to intertemporal substitution between current and future real consumption

quiz.question: Expected long-run real GDP growth 상승의 전형적 real short-rate 방향은?
quiz.explanation: Future income 기대가 높아지면 current consumption 선호와 saving response를 통해 real rate 상승 압력이 생길 수 있어.

quiz.question: Growth volatility 상승이 real rates에 줄 수 있는 영향은?
quiz.explanation: Uncertainty가 커지면 required compensation이 커질 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 34.d
losItems.title: Business cycle, policy rates and yield-curve shape
losItems.losText: Business-cycle phase가 monetary policy, short rates, yield-curve slope, maturity별 bond relative performance에 미치는 영향을 설명해.
losItems.why: Recession/expansion을 rate level만으로 외우지 않고 curve slope와 duration performance로 연결하는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Business-cycle phase가 monetary policy, short rates, yield-curve slope, maturity별 bond relative performance에 미치는 영향을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Recession에서는 policy rates가 낮은 경우가 많고 recovery 기대가 생기면 long rates가 상대적으로 높아 upward slope가 나타날 수 있어. Late expansion에서는 tight policy와 high short rates로 inversion 가능성이 커질 수 있어.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Short nominal rate는 real rate+expected inflation에 가깝고, long rate에는 inflation uncertainty/term premium이 더해진다. Business cycle과 policy reaction function이 short end를, long-run growth/inflation expectations가 long end를 다르게 움직인다.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0472~N0473의 메모는 inflation/rates와 policy-cycle을 연결했어. Bear/bull + steepening/flattening을 분리해서 판단하도록 구성했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Policy rate와 10Y yield 동일 폭 이동

formulas: Term spread = long-maturity yield − short-maturity yield
Short nominal rate ≈ real rate + expected inflation

quiz.question: Economy가 recession에서 막 회복되기 시작하고 policy rate가 낮다면 전형적 curve는?
quiz.explanation: Future growth/inflation 기대 때문에 long rates가 higher일 수 있어.

quiz.question: Late expansion에서 short rates가 high, long rates가 future slowdown을 반영하면?
quiz.explanation: Tight policy가 short end를 높일 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 34.e
losItems.title: Break-even inflation and inflation-risk premium
losItems.losText: 같은 maturity의 nominal government bond와 inflation-indexed bond yield spread를 구성하는 expected inflation과 inflation-uncertainty premium을 설명해.
losItems.why: BEI를 “시장 기대 inflation” 하나로 단정하는 함정을 막는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: 같은 maturity의 nominal government bond와 inflation-indexed bond yield spread를 구성하는 expected inflation과 inflation-uncertainty premium을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Nominal bond yield는 real yield + expected inflation + inflation uncertainty premium을 반영할 수 있어. Inflation-indexed bond는 실질 purchasing power를 보호하므로 두 yield 차이는 break-even inflation의 시장가격 정보를 줘.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Observed BEI에는 expected inflation뿐 아니라 inflation risk premium, relative liquidity, tax/technical differences가 섞일 수 있어. 따라서 survey expectation과 동일하지 않다.

sections.title: 4. 계산·판단 workflow
bullets: Same maturity nominal vs indexed yield

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0472는 real vs nominal rates와 inflation uncertainty를 정리했어. 단순 BEI=expected inflation이 아니라 premium을 분리하는 trap으로 흡수했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Indexed bond에 liquidity premium이 없다고 가정
Real yield와 nominal yield sign 혼동

formulas: BEI = nominal yield − inflation-indexed real yield
Conceptually: BEI ≈ expected inflation + inflation uncertainty premium + market technical effects

quiz.question: Nominal 4.2%, indexed real yield 1.5%면 BEI는?
BEI를 expected inflation과 완전히 동일시하기 어려운 이유는?
quiz.explanation: Observed spread에는 risk/market technicals가 섞일 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 34.f
losItems.title: Business cycle and credit spreads
losItems.losText: Business-cycle phase가 credit spreads와 credit-sensitive fixed-income instruments의 relative performance에 미치는 영향을 설명해.
losItems.why: Downturn에서 default와 recovery가 동시에 악화될 수 있다는 점을 spread와 rating tier performance로 연결해야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Business-cycle phase가 credit spreads와 credit-sensitive fixed-income instruments의 relative performance에 미치는 영향을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Expansion에서는 default concern이 낮아지고 spreads가 narrow해질 수 있어 lower-quality credit가 더 크게 outperform할 수 있다. Downturn에서는 spread widening과 recovery deterioration로 lower-quality가 더 크게 underperform할 수 있어.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Credit spread는 default-free benchmark 대비 compensation이야. Cycle weakens → expected PD↑, recovery↓, liquidity premium↑ 가능. Price sensitivity는 spread duration과 spread change에 좌우돼.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0472의 inflation/rate note를 credit-sensitive bond performance로 확장했고 N0475의 macro regime 메모와 연결했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Spread widening과 benchmark rate 상승을 동일시
Credit spread만 보고 total return 확정

formulas: Credit spread = risky yield − default-free benchmark yield
Approx. ΔP/P ≈ −spread duration × Δspread

quiz.question: Economic downturn의 전형적 credit spread 방향은?
quiz.explanation: PD, LGD, liquidity concerns가 커질 수 있어.

quiz.question: Spreads가 크게 narrow될 때 상대적으로 더 benefit 가능성이 큰 것은?
quiz.explanation: Higher initial spreads와 spread sensitivity 때문에 lower-quality가 더 크게 outperform할 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 34.g
losItems.title: Product-market characteristics and corporate credit quality
losItems.losText: Company product market의 cyclicality, competition, demand stability, pricing power 등이 corporate credit quality에 미치는 영향을 설명해.
losItems.why: 같은 leverage라도 consumer staples와 highly cyclical durable-goods issuer의 credit risk가 다른 이유를 묻는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Company product market의 cyclicality, competition, demand stability, pricing power 등이 corporate credit quality에 미치는 영향을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Stable essential demand, diversified customers, strong pricing power는 CF volatility를 낮춰 debt-service capacity를 지지한다. Cyclical/discretionary demand, high operating leverage, rapid obsolescence는 downturn loss를 키울 수 있어.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Product-market risk는 revenue volatility와 margins를 통해 EBITDA, interest coverage, leverage path, recovery value를 바꿔 credit spread에 반영된다. Industry structure와 competitive intensity도 중요해.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0473은 earnings cycle과 industry sensitivity를 연결했어. Consumer cyclical vs non-cyclical spread behavior를 credit-quality checklist로 재구성했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Market share만 보고 cash-flow stability 확정

formulas: Credit quality improves with more stable and resilient operating cash flows, all else equal

quiz.question: Downturn에서 credit spread가 더 크게 widen하기 쉬운 sector는?
quiz.explanation: Cyclical demand와 operating leverage가 CF stress를 키워.

quiz.question: Strong pricing power의 credit effect는?
quiz.explanation: Input-cost shock를 price에 전가할 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 34.h
losItems.title: Consumption hedging and the equity risk premium
losItems.losText: Equity가 bad-consumption states를 얼마나 hedge하는지가 equity risk premium과 어떤 관계를 갖는지 설명해.
losItems.why: 왜 “경기 좋을 때 같이 오르는 asset”이 높은 expected return을 요구하는지 consumption-based intuition을 묻는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Equity가 bad-consumption states를 얼마나 hedge하는지가 equity risk premium과 어떤 관계를 갖는지 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Investor는 marginal utility가 높은 bad times에 payoff를 주는 asset을 더 가치 있게 봐서 낮은 risk premium도 수용해. Equity는 대체로 good times에 payoff가 높고 bad times에 낮아 poor consumption hedge이므로 positive ERP를 요구받는다.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Pricing kernel/marginal utility와 asset payoff covariance가 핵심. Payoff가 marginal utility와 positive covariance면 hedge asset, negative covariance면 procyclical risky asset에 가까워 required premium이 높아진다.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0473~N0474는 earnings cycle과 valuation premium을 묶었어. Equity가 bad-consumption state에서 약한 이유를 ERP와 연결했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Equity가 dividend를 주므로 good hedge라고 단정

formulas: Better bad-state payoff → lower required risk premium, all else equal

quiz.question: Bad economic states에서 payoff가 높은 asset의 전형적 risk premium은?
quiz.explanation: High marginal-utility states에서 pay하면 hedge value가 있어.

quiz.question: Broad equity가 positive ERP를 요구받는 consumption-based 이유는?

losItems.label: LOS 34.i
losItems.title: Business cycle and earnings-growth expectations
losItems.losText: Business-cycle phase가 short-term과 long-term corporate earnings growth expectations를 어떻게 바꾸는지 설명해.
losItems.why: Cyclical earnings rebound와 sustainable long-run growth를 분리해야 valuation이 안 왜곡돼.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Business-cycle phase가 short-term과 long-term corporate earnings growth expectations를 어떻게 바꾸는지 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Early recovery에서는 depressed base와 operating leverage로 short-term earnings growth가 매우 높아질 수 있어. Late expansion에서는 margins/earnings가 peak에 가까워 growth expectations가 둔화될 수 있다. Long-run growth는 trend productivity, demographics, competitive position에 더 좌우돼.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Cyclical industries는 revenue/earnings beta가 높고 defensive industries는 상대적으로 stable. Analysts’ short-term revisions가 long-term terminal growth로 그대로 이어지면 valuation error가 커진다.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0474~N0475는 growth, valuation multiples, macro regime를 함께 봤어. Short-term rebound를 terminal growth로 extrapolate하지 않는 trap을 강조했어.

formulas: Short-run earnings growth can be highly cyclical; long-run growth should be tied to sustainable fundamentals

quiz.question: Early recovery에서 cyclical company short-term EPS growth가 매우 높을 수 있는 이유는?
quiz.explanation: Depressed base와 fixed-cost leverage가 rebound를 키워.

quiz.question: 가장 위험한 valuation practice는?
choices: Cyclical rebound growth를 terminal growth로 연장
quiz.explanation: Temporary rebound를 영구성장으로 놓으면 과대평가 가능성이 커.

losItems.label: LOS 34.j
losItems.title: Cyclical effects on valuation multiples
losItems.losText: Business cycle이 expected growth와 required return을 바꾸면서 P/E 등 valuation multiples에 어떤 cyclical effect를 주는지 설명해.
losItems.why: Historical average multiple만으로 over/undervaluation을 판단하면 왜 위험한지 묻는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Business cycle이 expected growth와 required return을 바꾸면서 P/E 등 valuation multiples에 어떤 cyclical effect를 주는지 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Multiples는 expected growth가 높고 required return이 낮을수록 높아지는 경향이 있어. Expansion에서는 optimism/ERP decline으로 multiple expansion, recession에서는 growth revision down/ERP up으로 compression 가능.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: But cycle turning points에서 E denominator가 depressed/peaked되어 trailing P/E가 역설적으로 보일 수 있어. CAPE처럼 normalized earnings를 쓰는 이유도 cyclical denominator noise를 줄이기 위해서야.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0474~N0475는 growth·real rates·multiples를 한 묶음으로 정리했어. “낮은 P/E=싸다”와 동일하게 “높은 P/E=비싸다”를 cycle adjustment 없이 쓰지 않도록 했어.

formulas: Justified multiples generally ↑ with expected growth and ↓ with required return

quiz.question: Expansion에서 ERP가 하락하고 growth expectation이 상승하면 multiples는?
quiz.explanation: Both effects support higher justified multiples.

quiz.question: Recession trough에서 trailing P/E가 높아질 수 있는 이유는?
quiz.explanation: Depressed E 때문에 P/E가 mechanically high할 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 34.k
losItems.title: Commercial real-estate macro drivers
losItems.losText: Commercial real estate가 bond-like income과 equity-like terminal value를 동시에 가지며, growth·rates·tenant credit·supply/demand·illiquidity가 valuation에 미치는 영향을 설명해.
losItems.why: R31 real estate valuation과 macro cycle을 연결하는 bridge LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Commercial real estate가 bond-like income과 equity-like terminal value를 동시에 가지며, growth·rates·tenant credit·supply/demand·illiquidity가 valuation에 미치는 영향을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Rental income stream은 bond-like, reversion/terminal property value는 equity-like. Tenant quality는 credit risk, occupancy/rent growth는 business cycle, cap rates는 interest rates와 required return에 민감해.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Commercial property discount rate에는 real rate, inflation expectations, uncertainty, credit/terminal-value risk, illiquidity premium이 반영될 수 있다. Construction lags 때문에 supply cycle이 business cycle보다 느리게 조정될 수 있어.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0475는 macro regimes와 asset class implications를, earlier real-estate notes는 rental income·terminal value·illiquidity를 연결했어.

formulas: Commercial RE discount rate can be viewed as base rates + inflation/risk components + terminal-value and illiquidity premiums

quiz.question: Commercial RE의 bond-like characteristic는?
quiz.explanation: Lease rents는 bond-like CF 성격을 줘.

quiz.question: Commercial RE에 추가로 요구될 수 있는 premium은?
quiz.explanation: 거래가 느리고 costly해서 illiquidity compensation이 중요해.

readings.title: Analysis of Active Portfolio Management
readings.sourceNote: 2026 Schweser Book5 backbone + Marie note ledger N0476~N0480, independently rewritten for study use.
readings.overview: Active weights와 value added에서 IR, fundamental law, manager selection, active-risk budgeting까지 이어진다.
readings.examPriority: 매우 높음. IR, IC, breadth, TC, active risk 관계와 strategy-change interpretation이 핵심이다.

losItems.label: LOS 35.a
losItems.title: Measuring value added by active management
losItems.losText: Active weights와 benchmark-relative expected/realized returns를 이용해 active management의 value added를 측정하는 방법을 설명해.
losItems.why: Active management는 portfolio return level이 아니라 benchmark 대비 어떤 decisions가 value를 만들었는지 봐야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Active weights와 benchmark-relative expected/realized returns를 이용해 active management의 value added를 측정하는 방법을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Active weight는 portfolio weight−benchmark weight이고 합은 0이야. Active return은 portfolio return−benchmark return. Security selection과 allocation effects를 benchmark-relative framework에서 분해할 수 있어.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Expected active return은 active weights와 expected returns의 결합으로 계산해. Benchmark weights 자체가 active decision이 아니며, value added는 asset allocation, security selection, interaction 등 attribution structure로 볼 수 있다.

sections.title: 4. 계산·판단 workflow
bullets: Costs 차감 후 net value added

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0476은 benchmark quality, active return, active weights를 정리했어. 특히 active weights 합이 0이라는 sanity check를 유지했어.

formulas: w_A,i = w_P,i − w_B,i
R_A = R_P − R_B
Expected active return = Σ w_A,i × E(R_i) under a simple decomposition

quiz.question: Portfolio 8%, benchmark 6%면 active return은?
Fully invested portfolio와 benchmark의 active weights 합은?

losItems.label: LOS 35.b
losItems.title: Information ratio versus Sharpe ratio
losItems.losText: Ex post와 ex ante information ratio를 계산·해석하고 Sharpe ratio와 비교해.
losItems.why: IR은 benchmark-relative skill efficiency, Sharpe는 cash/risk-free-relative total portfolio efficiency라는 구분이 핵심이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Ex post와 ex ante information ratio를 계산·해석하고 Sharpe ratio와 비교해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: IR=active return/tracking risk. Ex ante는 expected active return/expected active risk, ex post는 realized mean active return/realized tracking error. Sharpe=(portfolio return−risk-free)/total volatility.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: High IR은 active risk 1단위당 excess benchmark return이 높다는 뜻. 두 portfolios가 같은 Sharpe라도 benchmark choice와 active risk efficiency는 다를 수 있어.

sections.title: 4. 계산·판단 workflow
bullets: Ex ante vs ex post 확인

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0476~N0477은 active return, tracking error, IR을 같은 표로 묶었고 ex ante/ex post 차이를 강조했어.

formulas: IR_ex ante = E(R_P−R_B)/σ(R_P−R_B)
IR_ex post = mean realized active return / realized tracking error
Sharpe = (R_P−R_f)/σ_P

quiz.question: Active return 3%, tracking error 6%면 IR은?
Sharpe와 IR의 가장 큰 차이는?
choices: Total risk vs benchmark-relative active risk
quiz.explanation: Sharpe는 total portfolio, IR은 active management 효율성이야.

losItems.label: LOS 35.c
losItems.title: Fundamental law of active management
losItems.losText: Information coefficient, breadth, transfer coefficient, active risk를 이용해 fundamental law를 설명·해석해.
losItems.why: “Skill × opportunities × implementation”이 IR을 만든다는 R35 핵심 공식이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Information coefficient, breadth, transfer coefficient, active risk를 이용해 fundamental law를 설명·해석해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: IC는 forecast skill, breadth는 effectively independent bets, TC는 unconstrained optimal positions가 actual portfolio로 얼마나 전달되는지, active risk는 aggressiveness를 나타내.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Basic law IR≈IC√BR. Constraints를 포함한 full form에서는 TC가 곱해져 IR≈TC×IC×√BR. Bets 간 correlation이 높으면 nominal number of positions보다 effective breadth가 작아.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0477~N0478은 IC·breadth·TC와 IR을 직접 연결했고, correlated bets는 breadth를 줄인다는 메모를 살렸어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Active risk를 IR formula 안에 무조건 곱함

formulas: Basic: IR ≈ IC × √BR
Full: IR ≈ TC × IC × √BR
Expected active return ≈ IR × active risk

quiz.question: IC=0.05, breadth=100, TC=1이면 approximate IR은?
Constraints가 severe해 TC가 낮아지면?

losItems.label: LOS 35.d
losItems.title: Manager selection and optimal active risk
losItems.losText: Information ratio를 manager selection과 active risk level 결정에 어떻게 활용하는지 설명해.
losItems.why: High-alpha manager보다 consistent risk-adjusted active skill manager를 고르는 사고를 묻는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Information ratio를 manager selection과 active risk level 결정에 어떻게 활용하는지 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Given stable expected IR, more active risk raises expected active return linearly but also risk. Investor objective and benchmark risk determine optimal aggressiveness. Manager selection에서는 expected IR persistence, process, capacity, correlation with other managers를 봐.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Combining managers requires active-return correlation. Two high-IR managers with identical factor bets may add little diversification. Sharpe-maximizing aggressiveness depends on benchmark Sharpe, active-return correlation with benchmark, and IR.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0478~N0479는 fundamental law에서 manager skill과 optimal active risk로 넘어갔어. Raw active return보다 persistent IR을 보라는 메모를 유지했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Highest historical active return manager 선택

formulas: E(active return)=IR×active risk
Portfolio combination should account for correlations among active returns

quiz.question: 같은 expected IR에서 active risk를 2배로 하면 expected active return은 단순 관계상?
두 high-IR managers의 alpha가 highly correlated라면?
quiz.explanation: 같은 active bets라면 combined breadth가 작아.

losItems.label: LOS 35.e
losItems.title: Comparing active strategies through the fundamental law
losItems.losText: Market timing, security selection 등 active strategies를 비교하고 strategy changes가 IC, breadth, TC, active risk에 미치는 영향을 평가해.
losItems.why: 새 strategy가 “더 많은 bets”처럼 보여도 skill/independence/implementation이 나빠지면 IR이 하락할 수 있어.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Market timing, security selection 등 active strategies를 비교하고 strategy changes가 IC, breadth, TC, active risk에 미치는 영향을 평가해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Market timing은 fewer broad bets with potentially larger payoff but low breadth, security selection은 many smaller bets with broader opportunity set일 수 있어. Quant constraints, turnover caps, leverage limits는 TC를 바꾼다.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Strategy change는 each component separately 분석해야 해: new signals may improve IC, expand independent universe → BR↑, but tighter constraints → TC↓, crowding/correlation → effective BR↓.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0479~N0480은 optimal active risk와 constraints/risk budgeting을 묶었어. Strategy change를 fundamental-law components로 진단하도록 재구성했어.

formulas: Expected IR ≈ TC×IC×√BR
Strategy comparison should evaluate component changes, not only backtest return

quiz.question: Universe를 2배로 늘렸지만 bets가 highly correlated면 effective breadth는?
New compliance constraint가 optimal positions를 막으면 주로 어떤 term이 하락?

losItems.label: LOS 35.f
losItems.title: Strengths and limitations of the fundamental law
losItems.losText: Fundamental law를 실무에 적용할 때의 strengths와 limitations를 설명해.
losItems.why: 공식을 계산하는 것보다 assumptions가 깨지는 상황을 아는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Fundamental law를 실무에 적용할 때의 strengths와 limitations를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Strength는 skill, opportunities, implementation을 분리해 active process를 진단하고 manager/strategy comparison framework를 제공한다는 점이야.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Limitations: IC와 breadth estimation error, nonstationary skill, correlated bets, transaction costs, nonlinear constraints, time-varying TC, capacity/crowding, tail risk. Ex post fitting은 overstate할 수 있어.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0480의 active-risk budgeting/constraints 메모를 full-law limitation으로 연결했어. “formula가 neat하다고 world가 stationary한 건 아니다”가 핵심이야.

formulas: Fundamental law is a diagnostic approximation, not a guarantee of future IR

quiz.question: Fundamental law의 practical limitation이 아닌 것은?
quiz.explanation: Guaranteed future alpha는 strength가 아니라 존재하지 않는 가정이야.

quiz.question: Nominal 500 positions인데 common factor exposure가 강하면?
choices: Effective breadth가 훨씬 작을 수 있음

readings.title: Exchange-Traded Funds: Mechanics and Applications
readings.sourceNote: 2026 Schweser Book5 backbone + Marie note ledger N0440~N0445, independently rewritten for study use.
readings.overview: Creation/redemption mechanism, secondary liquidity, tracking, spreads, NAV gaps, total cost, risk와 portfolio applications를 다룬다.
readings.examPriority: 높음. AP arbitrage direction, tracking error vs tracking difference, bid-ask determinants, leveraged ETF path dependence가 중요하다.

losItems.label: LOS 36.a
losItems.title: ETF creation/redemption and authorized participants
losItems.losText: ETF creation/redemption mechanism과 authorized participant(AP)의 기능을 설명해.
losItems.why: ETF가 closed-end fund처럼 persistent premium/discount에 갇히지 않는 핵심 구조야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: ETF creation/redemption mechanism과 authorized participant(AP)의 기능을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Retail investor는 exchange에서 ETF shares를 trade하고, AP는 primary market에서 large creation units를 fund와 create/redeem해. In-kind basket delivery가 흔해.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: ETF premium이면 AP가 underlying basket을 사서 ETF를 create 후 sell할 유인이 있고, discount면 ETF를 buy/redeem해 basket을 받아 sell할 유인이 있다. Arbitrage band는 costs/liquidity에 의해 결정돼.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0440은 secondary market와 AP primary market을 분리하고 in-kind basket의 tax/transaction efficiency를 정리했어.

formulas: Premium arbitrage intuition: buy basket → create ETF → sell ETF
Discount intuition: buy ETF → redeem → sell basket

quiz.question: ETF가 NAV보다 premium에 거래될 때 AP의 전형적 행동은?
choices: Buy basket, create ETF, sell ETF

quiz.question: Retail investor의 일반적 ETF 거래 장소는?

losItems.label: LOS 36.b
losItems.title: How ETFs trade in secondary markets
losItems.losText: ETF의 intraday secondary-market trading, limit/market orders, liquidity sources와 market-making 구조를 설명해.
losItems.why: Displayed ETF volume만 보고 liquidity를 판단하면 왜 위험한지 이해해야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: ETF의 intraday secondary-market trading, limit/market orders, liquidity sources와 market-making 구조를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: ETF는 주식처럼 intraday trade되고 bid/ask quotes가 있어. Liquidity는 ETF shares 자체의 secondary volume뿐 아니라 underlying basket liquidity와 AP create/redeem capacity에서 온다.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Market makers hedge ETF inventory with underlying securities/futures. NAV publication timing, international market closure, stale prices는 market price와 calculated NAV 차이를 키울 수 있다.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0440~N0442는 secondary-market trading과 AP arbitrage를 연결하고 stale NAV/market closure를 premium-discount source로 정리했어.

formulas: ETF liquidity = secondary-market liquidity + access to primary-market creation/redemption liquidity

quiz.question: ETF displayed volume이 낮아도 liquid할 수 있는 이유는?
International ETF price-NAV gap이 커질 수 있는 상황은?
choices: Underlying market closed while ETF market open

losItems.label: LOS 36.c
losItems.title: Sources of ETF tracking error
losItems.losText: ETF가 benchmark return을 정확히 복제하지 못하는 tracking error의 원인을 설명해.
losItems.why: Fee만 외우면 부족해. Sampling, taxes, cash drag, reconstitution, lending까지 구분해야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: ETF가 benchmark return을 정확히 복제하지 못하는 tracking error의 원인을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Tracking difference는 ETF return−index return의 average bias, tracking error는 그 difference의 variability로 볼 수 있어.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Sources: expense ratio, sampling/optimization, transaction costs, index rebalancing timing, withholding taxes, cash balances, futures roll, securities lending revenue, corporate actions, implementation delays.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0441~N0443은 fees·sampling·rebalancing·tax·cash drag·lending을 한 번에 정리했어.

formulas: Tracking difference_t = R_ETF,t − R_Index,t
Tracking error = std dev of tracking differences

quiz.question: ETF return−index return의 volatility는?
Tracking error source가 아닌 것은?
quiz.explanation: Ticker length는 경제적 tracking source가 아니야.

losItems.label: LOS 36.d
losItems.title: Determinants of ETF bid–ask spreads
losItems.losText: ETF bid–ask spread를 결정하는 underlying liquidity, volatility, creation/redemption cost, competition, market hours 등의 요인을 설명해.
losItems.why: Expense ratio는 annual holding cost이고 bid-ask spread는 trading cost라는 구분도 중요해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: ETF bid–ask spread를 결정하는 underlying liquidity, volatility, creation/redemption cost, competition, market hours 등의 요인을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Market maker는 inventory/hedging/adverse-selection costs를 spread에 반영한다. Underlying securities가 illiquid/volatile하면 hedge가 어렵고 spread가 widen되기 쉽다.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Other factors: ETF AUM/volume, number of market makers, basket complexity, market-hours overlap, creation fees, taxes, settlement risk, stressed markets.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0441~N0443은 tracking과 premium/discount notes를 spread determinants까지 연결했어. N0442의 stale NAV 그림은 market-hours mismatch risk를 보여줘.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Expense ratio와 bid-ask spread 합산 중복
Absolute dollar spread를 different price ETFs와 직접 비교

formulas: Relative spread ≈ (Ask−Bid)/Mid × 10,000 bp

quiz.question: Underlying bonds가 illiquid해지면 ETF spread는?
ETF price $20, bid 19.98 ask 20.02. spread는 대략?

losItems.label: LOS 36.e
losItems.title: Sources of ETF premiums and discounts to NAV
losItems.losText: ETF market price가 NAV 대비 premium/discount를 보이는 원인과 arbitrage가 gap을 완전히 제거하지 못하는 상황을 설명해.
losItems.why: Premium/discount가 곧 mispricing이라는 단순화에서 벗어나 stale NAV와 underlying price discovery를 이해해야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: ETF market price가 NAV 대비 premium/discount를 보이는 원인과 arbitrage가 gap을 완전히 제거하지 못하는 상황을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Creation/redemption arbitrage가 gap을 제한하지만 transaction costs, illiquid basket, AP balance-sheet constraints, market closures, stale NAV, settlement/tax frictions로 gap이 지속될 수 있어.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Stress periods에는 ETF price가 underlying stale marks보다 더 빠른 price discovery를 제공할 수도 있어. 따라서 apparent discount가 반드시 ETF가 싸다는 뜻은 아니야.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0441~N0443은 premium/discount와 AP arbitrage, stale NAV를 직접 연결했어.

formulas: Premium/discount = (Market price − NAV)/NAV

quiz.question: ETF discount가 apparent해도 true mispricing이 아닐 수 있는 이유는?
quiz.explanation: ETF market price가 더 최신 information을 반영할 수 있어.

quiz.question: AP arbitrage가 약해지는 상황은?

losItems.label: LOS 36.f
losItems.title: Total costs of owning an ETF
losItems.losText: ETF ownership cost를 explicit fees뿐 아니라 trading, tracking, tax, market-impact costs까지 포함해 설명해.
losItems.why: 가장 낮은 expense ratio ETF가 항상 cheapest가 아닌 이유를 묻는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: ETF ownership cost를 explicit fees뿐 아니라 trading, tracking, tax, market-impact costs까지 포함해 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Costs include management fee, bid-ask spread, brokerage, market impact, premium/discount at entry/exit, tracking difference, taxes, lending/derivatives effects.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Short holding period and frequent trading에서는 spread/impact가 dominant할 수 있고, long horizon에서는 expense ratio와 tax efficiency가 중요해질 수 있어.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0444는 leveraged/inverse risks와 함께 implementation cost를 다뤘어. Site에서는 total-cost-of-ownership checklist로 분리했어.

formulas: TCO ≈ expense fees + trading costs + market impact + tracking/tax effects

quiz.question: 1주일만 보유하는 large ETF trade에서 dominant cost가 될 수 있는 것은?
Expense ratio가 가장 낮은 ETF가 always cheapest인가?
quiz.explanation: Tracking, spread, tax, impact를 함께 봐야 해.

losItems.label: LOS 36.g
losItems.title: Types of ETF risk
losItems.losText: ETF의 market, liquidity, tracking, counterparty, concentration, structural, closure, leveraged/inverse path-dependence risks를 설명해.
losItems.why: ETF wrapper가 underlying risk를 없애지 않는다는 점이 핵심이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: ETF의 market, liquidity, tracking, counterparty, concentration, structural, closure, leveraged/inverse path-dependence risks를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: ETF risk는 underlying market risk 외에도 creation/redemption disruption, derivatives counterparty, securities lending, concentration, tracking, trading liquidity, premium/discount, closure risk가 있다.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Leveraged/inverse ETFs는 daily target return을 추구해 compounding path dependence가 크다. Multi-day return은 simple leverage multiple과 달라질 수 있다.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0444는 leveraged/inverse daily reset와 compounding path dependence를 핵심 trap으로 적었어.

formulas: Leveraged ETF multi-period return depends on return path due to daily reset

quiz.question: Daily 2x ETF의 1개월 return은 benchmark 1개월 return의 정확히 2배인가?
ETF wrapper가 제거하지 않는 risk는?

losItems.label: LOS 36.h
losItems.title: Portfolio applications of ETFs
losItems.losText: ETF를 strategic exposure, tactical allocation, cash equitization, transition management, completion, hedging, factor implementation 등에 활용하는 방법을 식별·설명해.
losItems.why: ETF selection을 product shopping이 아니라 portfolio problem solving으로 연결하는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: ETF를 strategic exposure, tactical allocation, cash equitization, transition management, completion, hedging, factor implementation 등에 활용하는 방법을 식별·설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: ETFs provide rapid, transparent, scalable exposure. Common uses: core beta, tactical over/underweights, interim exposure while manager search, equitize cash, completion portfolios, rebalancing, hedging, liquidity sleeves.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Choice depends on tracking quality, liquidity, taxes, basis risk, underlying fit, derivatives alternatives, holding horizon. ETF is not automatically superior to futures or swaps.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0445는 strategic beta, tactical allocation, cash equitization, transition/completion, hedging을 한 장에 정리했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Product label만 보고 exposure purity 가정

formulas: Use-case selection is a cost/exposure/implementation decision, not just expense-ratio ranking

quiz.question: Manager transition 동안 market exposure를 유지하는 활용은?
quiz.explanation: Temporary ETF exposure로 cash drag를 줄일 수 있어.

quiz.question: ETF vs futures 선택에서 고려할 것은?
choices: Horizon, liquidity, tax, basis and operational constraints

readings.title: Using Multifactor Models
readings.sourceNote: 2026 Schweser Book5 backbone + Marie note ledger N0446~N0453; LOS 37.f~37.g additionally filled from 2026 backbone because no direct note-image mapping.
readings.overview: APT에서 factor-model taxonomy, expected return, risk decomposition, active exposures와 multiple risk dimensions까지 연결한다.
readings.examPriority: 매우 높음. APT arbitrage logic, factor-premium calculation, model-family comparison, factor/specific risk decomposition이 핵심이다.

losItems.label: LOS 37.a
losItems.title: APT foundations and link to multifactor models
losItems.losText: Arbitrage Pricing Theory의 assumptions와 multifactor return model과의 관계를 설명해.
losItems.why: CAPM single beta를 넘어 여러 systematic risk dimensions가 expected return을 설명한다는 출발점이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Arbitrage Pricing Theory의 assumptions와 multifactor return model과의 관계를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: APT assumes asset returns have a factor structure, idiosyncratic risk can be diversified in a large portfolio, and persistent arbitrage cannot survive. Expected return depends on factor sensitivities and factor risk premia.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Unlike CAPM, APT does not require the market portfolio to be mean-variance efficient or specify exact economic factors. A practical multifactor model can use macro, fundamental or statistical factors.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0446은 APT assumptions와 expected-return equation을 정리했어. R37 전체 taxonomy의 출발점으로 썼어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Idiosyncratic risk earns premium in diversified equilibrium
Factors must be observable macro variables

formulas: E(R_i)=R_f + Σ β_{ik}λ_k (one common APT representation)

quiz.question: APT의 핵심 assumption 중 하나는?
APT가 CAPM보다 덜 요구하는 것은?
quiz.explanation: APT는 exact market portfolio framework에 덜 의존해.

losItems.label: LOS 37.b
losItems.title: Identifying arbitrage opportunities in factor models
losItems.losText: Factor-implied expected return과 observed/required return의 불일치에서 arbitrage opportunity가 존재하는지 판단해.
losItems.why: 단순 “expected return 높음=arbitrage”가 아니라 zero-net-investment factor-neutral portfolio를 구성할 수 있는지 보는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Factor-implied expected return과 observed/required return의 불일치에서 arbitrage opportunity가 존재하는지 판단해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: If two portfolios have same factor exposures but different expected returns, long higher-return and short lower-return can create factor-neutral profit. A mispriced asset can be combined with factor-mimicking portfolios to neutralize systematic exposures.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: True arbitrage ideally requires zero net investment and no risk/no loss across states. In empirical factor models, apparent alpha may reflect omitted factors, transaction costs or estimation error.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0446~N0450의 factor taxonomy와 risk decomposition을 이용해 “same exposures, different expected return” 비교 구조를 만들었어.

formulas: Mispricing alpha_i = E(R_i) − factor-implied expected return

quiz.question: 같은 factor exposures의 두 portfolios 중 expected return이 다르면?
quiz.explanation: Long high-return/short low-return로 systematic exposure를 neutralize할 수 있어.

quiz.question: Empirical alpha가 true arbitrage가 아닐 수 있는 이유는?

losItems.label: LOS 37.c
losItems.title: Expected return from factor sensitivities and premia
losItems.losText: Asset의 factor sensitivities와 factor risk premia가 주어졌을 때 expected return을 계산해.
losItems.why: R37의 가장 직접적인 계산 LOS야. Sensitivity와 premium의 부호를 정확히 처리해야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Asset의 factor sensitivities와 factor risk premia가 주어졌을 때 expected return을 계산해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Each factor contributes β×λ to expected return. Positive sensitivity to a positively priced factor raises required return; negative beta can reduce it. Intercept/risk-free convention depends on model specification.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Macroeconomic surprise models may express returns as expected return + factor sensitivities × factor surprises + residual. Expected-return equation uses premia, realized-return equation uses realized factor shocks.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0447~N0450와 N0453의 factor models/active decomposition notes를 calculation structure로 묶었어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Residual expected value를 arbitrary premium으로 넣음

formulas: E(R_i)=R_f + β_i1λ_1 + ... + β_iKλ_K

quiz.question: Positive-premium factor에 negative sensitivity면 contribution은?

losItems.label: LOS 37.d
losItems.title: Macroeconomic, fundamental and statistical factor models
losItems.losText: Macroeconomic, fundamental, statistical factor models의 construction, interpretation, strengths/weaknesses를 비교해.
losItems.why: 세 model family를 이름이 아니라 “factor가 어디서 오는가”로 구분해야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Macroeconomic, fundamental, statistical factor models의 construction, interpretation, strengths/weaknesses를 비교해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Macro models use economic surprises (growth, inflation, rates). Fundamental models use observable company characteristics (size, value, leverage, industry). Statistical models extract latent factors from return covariance, e.g., PCA.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Macro factors are intuitive but measurement/timing sensitive; fundamental models are implementable and attribution-friendly but definitions can be arbitrary; statistical factors fit covariance well but are hard to interpret and unstable across regimes.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0447~N0453은 three-model taxonomy와 factor-mimicking portfolios를 연결했어. N0448~N0449의 worked active decomposition도 포함했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Macro factor level vs surprise 혼동

formulas: Macro return form often: R_i = E(R_i)+Σβ_ik F_k + ε_i

quiz.question: PCA로 latent factors를 추출하는 것은?
Book-to-market 같은 company characteristic을 쓰는 것은?

losItems.label: LOS 37.e
losItems.title: Uses and interpretation of multifactor-model output
losItems.losText: Multifactor model의 outputs를 portfolio construction, risk decomposition, attribution, hedging, active exposure management에 활용하고 해석해.
losItems.why: Beta table을 보고 끝내는 게 아니라 실제 portfolio decision으로 연결하는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Multifactor model의 outputs를 portfolio construction, risk decomposition, attribution, hedging, active exposure management에 활용하고 해석해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Portfolio factor exposure is weighted aggregation of security exposures. Total/active risk can be decomposed into common factor covariance contribution and specific risk. Active performance can be decomposed into factor returns from active exposures plus residual selection.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Factor-mimicking portfolios isolate target exposure. Managers can hedge unwanted factor bets or intentionally tilt. But residual alpha may be contaminated by omitted factors.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0450~N0452는 b′Σ_Fb + specific risk variance, N0451은 factor vs security-specific alpha, N0452는 active risk formula를 정리했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Factor contributions 단순 합산 시 covariance 무시

formulas: Portfolio factor variance = b_P′Σ_F b_P
Total variance ≈ factor variance + specific variance
Active factor exposure = b_P − b_B

quiz.question: Portfolio factor exposures는 보통?
Residual alpha를 skill로 확정하기 전에 볼 것은?
quiz.explanation: Model이 중요한 systematic factor를 놓쳤을 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 37.f
losItems.title: Why multiple risk dimensions can help investors
losItems.losText: Asset returns를 여러 risk dimensions로 모델링할 때 투자자가 얻을 수 있는 잠재적 benefits를 설명해.
losItems.why: 이 LOS는 직접 필기 매핑이 없었기 때문에 2026 backbone을 기준으로 독립 보완한 항목이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Asset returns를 여러 risk dimensions로 모델링할 때 투자자가 얻을 수 있는 잠재적 benefits를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Single-factor view can hide concentration. Multiple dimensions reveal that two assets with similar market beta may differ in rates, inflation, credit, value, size, liquidity or other exposures.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Benefits include better diversification, more accurate risk budgeting, improved scenario analysis, intentional factor tilts, clearer attribution, hedging of unwanted exposures, and avoiding hidden common-factor concentration.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: 509장 노트에는 37.f 직접 대응 사진이 없어서, N0450~N0452의 factor/specific risk decomposition과 2026 LOS backbone을 연결해 빈칸 없이 작성했어.

formulas: Diversification benefit depends on covariance among factor exposures, not only number of holdings

quiz.question: 같은 market beta의 두 portfolios가 서로 다른 risk를 가질 수 있는 이유는?
More factors의 limitation은?
quiz.explanation: Dimension 증가가 model risk를 키울 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 37.g
losItems.title: Sources of active risk, tracking risk and information ratio
losItems.losText: Active risk의 sources를 설명하고 tracking risk와 information ratio를 multifactor context에서 해석해.
losItems.why: 이 LOS도 직접 note-image mapping이 없어서 backbone 기반으로 보완했어. R35와 R37을 연결하는 bridge야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Active risk의 sources를 설명하고 tracking risk와 information ratio를 multifactor context에서 해석해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Active return can come from active factor exposures and security-specific positions. Tracking risk likewise decomposes into factor active risk plus specific active risk, including covariance terms.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: High tracking risk may be intentional factor tilt or unintended concentration. IR evaluates active return per unit tracking risk, but a high historical IR can hide unstable factor exposures and omitted risks.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: Direct note mapping은 없지만 N0448~N0452의 active factor decomposition과 N0477~N0478의 IR/fundamental-law notes를 cross-topic으로 연결해 작성했어.

formulas: Tracking risk σ_A = std(R_P−R_B)
IR = E(R_P−R_B)/σ(R_P−R_B)
Active risk² ≈ active factor variance + active specific variance

quiz.question: Active risk의 source가 아닌 것은?
IR 0.6의 해석은?

readings.title: Measuring and Managing Market Risk
readings.sourceNote: 2026 Schweser Book5 backbone + Marie note ledger N0454~N0461, independently rewritten for study use.
readings.overview: VaR methods에서 ES, sensitivities, scenarios, limits, capital allocation, institution-specific risk measures까지 이어진다.
readings.examPriority: 최상. VaR interpretation, three methods, ES, scenario/sensitivity comparison, exposure measures와 limits가 반복 출제 포인트다.

losItems.label: LOS 38.a
losItems.title: Using Value at Risk to measure portfolio risk
losItems.losText: VaR의 horizon, confidence level, loss threshold 의미와 risk monitoring/limits/capital communication에서의 사용을 설명해.
losItems.why: “95% VaR 10m”을 maximum loss로 읽지 않는 게 첫 번째 trap이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: VaR의 horizon, confidence level, loss threshold 의미와 risk monitoring/limits/capital communication에서의 사용을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: VaR answers: over horizon H, at confidence c, what loss threshold is exceeded with probability 1−c under model assumptions? It summarizes tail quantile, not worst-case loss.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Uses include comparing desks, setting limits, regulatory/internal capital input, risk budgeting, and monitoring changes. It depends on positions, distribution, correlations, volatilities and horizon.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0454는 VaR definition, use, “not maximum loss”를 적었어. R38 전체의 anchor로 사용했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Profit quantile와 loss quantile sign 혼동

formulas: VaR_c,H = loss quantile such that P(Loss > VaR) ≈ 1−c

quiz.question: 1-day 99% VaR 5m의 가장 정확한 해석은?
VaR가 알려주지 않는 것은?
quiz.explanation: VaR 초과 후 얼마나 큰 손실인지 말하지 않아.

losItems.label: LOS 38.b
losItems.title: Comparing parametric, historical and Monte Carlo VaR
losItems.losText: Parametric, historical simulation, Monte Carlo VaR methods의 assumptions, strengths, weaknesses를 비교해.
losItems.why: Methods를 계산법만이 아니라 어떤 portfolio에 적합한지 구분해야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Parametric, historical simulation, Monte Carlo VaR methods의 assumptions, strengths, weaknesses를 비교해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Parametric uses estimated mean/variance/covariance and distributional form; historical replays actual past returns; Monte Carlo simulates from specified stochastic models.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Parametric is fast but weak for nonlinear/fat-tail portfolios. Historical preserves empirical joint moves but depends on window and assumes past relevance. Monte Carlo is flexible for nonlinear instruments but expensive and model-risk intensive.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0454~N0456와 N0460은 three-method comparison table을 만들었어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Different methods must give same VaR

formulas: Parametric VaR often uses μ, σ and z-quantile
Historical VaR = empirical loss quantile
Monte Carlo VaR = simulated loss quantile

quiz.question: Highly nonlinear options portfolio에 상대적으로 flexible한 method는?
quiz.explanation: Full revaluation under simulated paths가 가능해.

quiz.question: Historical simulation의 key limitation은?
choices: Past window may not represent future

losItems.label: LOS 38.c
losItems.title: Estimating and interpreting VaR
losItems.losText: Parametric, historical, Monte Carlo 방법으로 VaR를 실제 추정·해석해.
losItems.why: 38.b의 비교를 숫자로 실행하는 계산 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Parametric, historical, Monte Carlo 방법으로 VaR를 실제 추정·해석해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Parametric: portfolio mean/vol and quantile. Historical: order realized/simulated historical P&L and select tail percentile. Monte Carlo: simulate many joint risk-factor paths, revalue, take quantile.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Sign conventions matter. Many exam problems express VaR as positive loss. Horizon scaling by √T assumes iid/stable volatility and may fail under autocorrelation or nonlinear exposures.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0455~N0456와 N0460의 formulas/distribution diagrams를 계산 순서로 바꿨어.

formulas: Normal loss VaR ≈ z_c σ_P − μ_P (depending on sign convention)
Historical VaR = selected empirical tail loss
Monte Carlo VaR = selected simulated tail loss

quiz.question: Daily σ=2%, mean≈0, 95% one-tail z=1.645. Parametric VaR는 약?
Historical 99% VaR는?

losItems.label: LOS 38.d
losItems.title: Advantages and limitations of VaR
losItems.losText: VaR의 communication/aggregation strengths와 model risk, tail blindness, non-subadditivity 가능성, procyclicality 등 limitations를 설명해.
losItems.why: VaR가 유용하지만 하나의 숫자로 risk를 완전히 설명하지 못한다는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: VaR의 communication/aggregation strengths와 model risk, tail blindness, non-subadditivity 가능성, procyclicality 등 limitations를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Strengths: intuitive currency amount, cross-portfolio comparison, aggregation, limits/capital. Limitations: model/distribution error, unstable correlations, limited tail severity, liquidity omissions, nonlinear approximations, historical regime dependence.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Some VaR formulations can violate subadditivity, especially with non-elliptical distributions, so diversification benefit may be misrepresented. VaR can become procyclical as measured vol rises in stress.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0455~N0457는 methods와 limitations를 붙였고 N0460은 VaR vs stress-testing master note를 만들었어.

formulas: VaR does not measure average loss conditional on exceeding VaR

quiz.question: VaR의 대표적 limitation은?
quiz.explanation: Threshold beyond loss magnitude를 말하지 않아.

quiz.question: Stress period에 vol estimate가 급등하며 limits를 더 tighten시키는 현상은?

losItems.label: LOS 38.e
losItems.title: Extensions of VaR
losItems.losText: Expected shortfall, incremental/marginal/component VaR 등 VaR extensions를 설명해.
losItems.why: VaR threshold를 넘은 뒤의 severity와 portfolio contribution을 보완하는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Expected shortfall, incremental/marginal/component VaR 등 VaR extensions를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Expected shortfall(ES) is average loss conditional on being beyond VaR threshold. Incremental VaR looks at change in VaR when adding/removing position; marginal/component measures approximate risk contribution.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Extensions support capital allocation and tail analysis but still depend on models/data. ES is more tail-sensitive and coherent under broad conditions, though estimation error in extreme tails can be high.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0457은 expected shortfall과 tail severity, instability limitations를 강조했어.

formulas: ES_c = E[Loss | Loss > VaR_c] (continuous intuition)
Incremental VaR = VaR(new portfolio) − VaR(current)

quiz.question: VaR 초과 손실의 평균을 측정하는 것은?
Position 추가가 portfolio VaR를 얼마나 바꾸는지 보는 것은?

losItems.label: LOS 38.f
losItems.title: Sensitivity and scenario measures versus VaR
losItems.losText: Sensitivity risk measures와 scenario risk measures를 설명하고 VaR와 비교해.
losItems.why: Local Greeks/durations와 coherent multi-factor shocks를 probabilistic VaR와 구분해야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Sensitivity risk measures와 scenario risk measures를 설명하고 VaR와 비교해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Sensitivity measures approximate P&L for small risk-factor changes using delta, duration, DV01, beta, vega. Scenario measures revalue under specified historical/hypothetical multi-factor shocks. VaR attaches probability/confidence to loss quantile.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Sensitivities are transparent but local; scenarios capture nonlinear coherent moves but scenario selection may be subjective. VaR is broad but model-dependent and may miss chosen tail narratives.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0457~N0458는 ES, sensitivity, scenario, reverse stress를 비교했고 N0460은 master table로 정리했어.

formulas: Local P&L ≈ sensitivity × factor shock
Scenario P&L = full revaluation under specified joint shocks

quiz.question: DV01은 어떤 type?
quiz.explanation: Small yield change에 대한 local exposure야.

quiz.question: 2008-style joint market shock를 full revalue하는 것은?

losItems.label: LOS 38.g
losItems.title: Using equity, fixed-income and option exposure measures
losItems.losText: Equity, fixed-income, options exposure measures를 market/volatility risk 측정과 관리에 적용해.
losItems.why: Asset-class별 sensitivity를 공통 P&L language로 바꾸는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Equity, fixed-income, options exposure measures를 market/volatility risk 측정과 관리에 적용해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Equity: beta, factor exposures, delta. Fixed income: duration, DV01/PVBP, key-rate duration, spread duration. Options: delta, gamma, vega, theta and scenario revaluation.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Portfolio risk aggregation requires factor correlations and nonlinear effects. A delta-neutral option book can still have large gamma/vega risk; duration-neutral bond book can retain curve/spread risk.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0458의 stress notes와 earlier Book4 duration/Greeks notes를 cross-topic으로 연결했어.

formulas: Equity local P&L ≈ beta/factor exposure × factor move
Bond ΔP/P ≈ −D×Δy + 0.5 Conv×(Δy)^2
Option ΔV ≈ Δ×ΔS + 0.5Γ(ΔS)^2 + Vega×Δσ

quiz.question: Delta-neutral options portfolio에도 남을 수 있는 risk는?
Nonparallel yield curve risk에 유용한 것은?

losItems.label: LOS 38.h
losItems.title: Applying sensitivity and scenario measures
losItems.losText: Sensitivity/scenario measures를 limits, hedging, monitoring, stress-loss estimation에 실제 적용하는 방법을 설명해.
losItems.why: Measure를 계산한 뒤 management action으로 연결하는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Sensitivity/scenario measures를 limits, hedging, monitoring, stress-loss estimation에 실제 적용하는 방법을 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Sensitivities set exposure limits (DV01, delta, vega) and hedge ratios. Scenario analysis estimates portfolio loss under coherent shocks, identifies vulnerabilities and supports contingency planning.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Historical scenarios provide realism but may not match current positions/regime; hypothetical scenarios target current vulnerabilities. Reverse stress asks what shock set breaks a limit or solvency target.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0458은 sensitivity, scenario, reverse stress를 세 갈래로 정리했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Historical scenario 그대로 current portfolio에 valid

formulas: Hedge units ≈ target exposure / hedge instrument exposure

quiz.question: Portfolio DV01 limit 초과 시 전형적 action은?
Reverse stress의 질문은?

losItems.label: LOS 38.i
losItems.title: Advantages and limitations of sensitivity and scenario measures
losItems.losText: Sensitivity와 scenario risk measures의 장단점을 비교해.
losItems.why: VaR limitations을 보완하지만 각각 blind spot이 있다는 걸 정리하는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Sensitivity와 scenario risk measures의 장단점을 비교해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Sensitivity strengths: transparent, fast, actionable hedge units. Weaknesses: local linearity, ignores interactions, basis/second-order risk. Scenario strengths: coherent nonlinear full revaluation, narrative. Weaknesses: subjective selection, no probability, limited coverage.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Best practice uses complementary stack: sensitivities for daily control, VaR/ES for distribution, scenarios/reverse stress for tail narratives.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0458~N0460은 VaR-methods vs stress-testing master comparison을 만들었어.

formulas: Risk framework should combine complementary measures rather than rely on one metric

quiz.question: Sensitivity measure의 strength는?
Scenario analysis limitation은?

losItems.label: LOS 38.j
losItems.title: Market-risk constraints and limits
losItems.losText: Risk budgeting, position limits, scenario limits, stop-loss limits 등 market-risk constraints를 설명해.
losItems.why: Limit는 risk measure와 behavior를 연결하는 governance tool이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Risk budgeting, position limits, scenario limits, stop-loss limits 등 market-risk constraints를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Position limits cap notional/exposure, sensitivity limits cap DV01/delta/vega, VaR/scenario limits cap modeled loss, stop-loss limits trigger action after realized losses. Risk budgets allocate ex ante risk capacity.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Each limit can be gamed or miss risks. Position limits ignore risk per unit, VaR limits model-dependent, stop-loss procyclical, scenario limits incomplete. A layered framework is stronger.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0459~N0461은 position/sensitivity/VaR/scenario/stop-loss limits와 risk budgeting을 정리했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Limit breach 대응 없는 paper limit

formulas: Risk budget allocates allowable ex ante risk across units/strategies

quiz.question: Realized cumulative loss가 threshold를 넘으면 작동하는 limit은?
Position limit의 대표 limitation은?
choices: Same notional can have different risk
quiz.explanation: Risk per unit exposure가 다를 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 38.k
losItems.title: Using risk measures in capital allocation
losItems.losText: Risk measures를 business unit/strategy capital allocation decisions에 어떻게 활용하는지 설명해.
losItems.why: Raw P&L이 아니라 risk-adjusted capital consumption을 봐야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Risk measures를 business unit/strategy capital allocation decisions에 어떻게 활용하는지 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Capital can be allocated based on VaR/ES, stress loss, economic capital, marginal/incremental risk contribution. A strategy with high return but huge tail capital may be less attractive than lower-return efficient use of risk capital.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Diversification matters: standalone risk is not same as marginal contribution to firm-wide risk. Allocation should consider correlations, liquidity, constraints and strategic value.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0459~N0461은 capital allocation은 raw notional이 아니라 risk/diversification을 반영해야 한다고 적었어.

formulas: Marginal risk contribution = change in portfolio risk for small/additional exposure
Risk-adjusted return ≈ expected profit / allocated economic capital

quiz.question: Capital allocation에서 standalone risk보다 더 useful할 수 있는 것은?
High raw return strategy가 적은 capital을 받아야 할 수 있는 이유는?

losItems.label: LOS 38.l
losItems.title: Risk measures across banks, asset managers, pensions and insurers
losItems.losText: Banks, asset managers, pension funds, insurers가 각자의 liabilities, regulation, horizon에 맞춰 사용하는 risk measures를 설명해.
losItems.why: 같은 VaR라도 institution objective가 다르면 중요 risk가 달라진다는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Banks, asset managers, pension funds, insurers가 각자의 liabilities, regulation, horizon에 맞춰 사용하는 risk measures를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Banks emphasize trading VaR/ES, stress tests, liquidity/funding and capital. Asset managers focus tracking error, factor risk, drawdown, client limits. Pensions focus funded-status/asset-liability risk. Insurers focus solvency capital, duration/convexity mismatch, catastrophe and liability risks.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Horizon and liability structure drive measure choice. A pension’s key risk may be surplus volatility even when asset VaR is moderate; an insurer must model long-dated guarantees and policyholder behavior.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0459~N0461의 generic risk limits를 institution-specific use cases로 확장했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Tracking error key metric for bank solvency

formulas: Institutional risk should be measured relative to objectives and liabilities, not assets alone

quiz.question: Pension fund에 특히 중요한 risk는?
Asset manager active fund에 common measure는?

readings.title: Backtesting and Simulation
readings.sourceNote: 2026 Schweser Book5 backbone + Marie note ledger N0462~N0469, independently rewritten for study use.
readings.overview: Backtest objectives/process/metrics/biases와 historical scenario, Monte Carlo, simulation design, sensitivity analysis를 다룬다.
readings.examPriority: 높음. Look-ahead/survivorship/data snooping, point-in-time design, metrics interpretation, historical vs Monte Carlo와 sensitivity가 핵심이다.

losItems.label: LOS 39.a
losItems.title: Objectives of backtesting an investment strategy
losItems.losText: Investment strategy backtest의 objectives를 설명해.
losItems.why: Backtest는 “과거 수익률 자랑”이 아니라 mechanism, robustness, implementation risk를 검증하는 실험이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Investment strategy backtest의 objectives를 설명해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Objectives include evaluating economic logic, estimating return/risk distribution, understanding drawdowns, turnover/capacity, comparing alternatives, identifying regime dependence and implementation feasibility.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: A good backtest is falsifiable: rules are specified before evaluation and outputs are compared to benchmarks. It should support decision making, not manufacture a desired Sharpe ratio.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0462와 N0465는 process와 robustness metrics를 정리했어.

formulas: Backtest should evaluate both economic thesis and implementable performance

quiz.question: Backtest의 가장 건전한 objective는?
Good backtest가 포함해야 할 것은?
quiz.explanation: Comparative and ex ante specification이 중요해.

losItems.label: LOS 39.b
losItems.title: Steps and procedures in a backtest
losItems.losText: Investment strategy backtest의 주요 steps와 procedures를 설명·비교해.
losItems.why: Data 준비부터 rule simulation, costs, out-of-sample validation까지 순서를 묻는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Investment strategy backtest의 주요 steps와 procedures를 설명·비교해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Typical flow: define hypothesis → collect point-in-time data → clean/align → specify signals and portfolio rules → simulate rebalancing/execution → include costs → compute metrics → validate out-of-sample/robustness.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Procedures differ by rolling/expanding windows, cross-validation, walk-forward, rebalance timing, trade-price assumptions. Point-in-time constituent data is critical to avoid look-ahead/survivorship bias.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0462, N0465, N0469는 process, robustness, benchmark/model risk를 연결했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Same-day close signal and execution without lag

formulas: Net strategy return_t = gross simulated return_t − trading/financing/borrow costs_t

quiz.question: Look-ahead를 줄이는 핵심은?
quiz.explanation: 당시 알 수 있던 정보만 써야 해.

quiz.question: Final test set의 역할은?

losItems.label: LOS 39.c
losItems.title: Interpreting backtest metrics and visuals
losItems.losText: Backtest에서 보고되는 return, volatility, drawdown, hit rate, turnover, exposure, rolling metrics와 visualizations를 해석해.
losItems.why: Headline CAGR/Sharpe만 보고 전략 품질을 판단하지 않도록 하는 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Backtest에서 보고되는 return, volatility, drawdown, hit rate, turnover, exposure, rolling metrics와 visualizations를 해석해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Useful metrics: annualized return/volatility, Sharpe/Sortino, max drawdown, recovery time, downside capture, hit rate, turnover, capacity, skew/tails. Visuals: equity curve, underwater chart, rolling Sharpe/beta, exposure, distribution.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: A smooth equity curve can hide stale pricing or short-vol tail risk. High hit rate with rare catastrophic losses can be dangerous. Drawdown duration and path matter to investor utility.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0463~N0464와 N0467은 simulation paths와 output distributions를, N0465는 performance/robustness metrics를 다뤘어.

formulas: Sharpe = excess return / total volatility
Max drawdown = max peak-to-trough decline
Turnover informs transaction-cost/capacity burden

quiz.question: Rare huge losses가 있는 strategy는 어떤 metric이 특히 useful?
quiz.explanation: Hit rate가 높아도 tail loss가 치명적일 수 있어.

quiz.question: Underwater chart는 무엇을 보여줘?

losItems.label: LOS 39.d
losItems.title: Identifying problems in a backtest
losItems.losText: Look-ahead, survivorship, data snooping, overfitting, selection bias, unrealistic costs/liquidity 등 backtest problems를 식별해.
losItems.why: R39에서 가장 시험스러운 trap LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Look-ahead, survivorship, data snooping, overfitting, selection bias, unrealistic costs/liquidity 등 backtest problems를 식별해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Look-ahead uses unavailable information. Survivorship excludes failed names. Data snooping tests many rules then reports winner. Overfitting captures noise. Other issues: stale prices, benchmark mismatch, corporate-action errors, unrealistic fills, ignored shorting/borrow, nonstationarity.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: The more models/hyperparameters tried, the more likely a lucky result. True validation requires untouched data, economic rationale, robustness across parameters/subperiods and realistic execution.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0468~N0469는 look-ahead, survivorship, snooping, overfitting, structural breaks를 집중 정리했어.

formulas: No single formula fixes bias; prevention requires point-in-time design and independent validation

quiz.question: 현재 살아남은 stocks만 20년 backtest에 사용하는 bias는?
1,000 strategies 중 best Sharpe 하나만 보고하는 문제는?

losItems.label: LOS 39.e
losItems.title: Evaluating historical scenario analysis
losItems.losText: Historical scenario analysis의 construction과 outputs를 평가·해석해.
losItems.why: 과거 shock를 current portfolio에 replay하는 것이 단순 historical return lookup과 다르다는 점이 핵심이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Historical scenario analysis의 construction과 outputs를 평가·해석해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Historical scenario selects a past stress window and applies observed risk-factor moves to current positions, often with full revaluation. It preserves historically coherent joint moves.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Limitations: current portfolio/instruments differ, market structure/liquidity/regime changed, one event is not exhaustive. Results should be interpreted as conditional loss under that replay, not probability forecast.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0465, N0468, N0469의 robustness/model-risk notes를 historical scenario evaluation에 연결했어.

formulas: Historical scenario loss = current portfolio revaluation under selected historical factor shocks

quiz.question: 2008 factor shocks를 current portfolio에 적용하는 것은?
quiz.explanation: Past coherent shocks를 current exposures에 replay해.

quiz.question: Historical scenario loss 20m의 해석은?
choices: 그 shock replay 아래 conditional loss

losItems.label: LOS 39.f
losItems.title: Monte Carlo versus historical simulation
losItems.losText: Monte Carlo와 historical simulation의 assumptions, flexibility, strengths/weaknesses를 비교해.
losItems.why: R38 VaR와 겹치지만 R39에서는 strategy/backtest scenario generation 관점이 더 강해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Monte Carlo와 historical simulation의 assumptions, flexibility, strengths/weaknesses를 비교해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Historical simulation resamples/replays empirical observations and preserves empirical cross-asset moves. Monte Carlo generates synthetic paths from specified stochastic models, enabling unseen scenarios and nonlinear path analysis.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Historical is data-realistic but sample/regime limited. Monte Carlo is flexible but model-dependent. Bootstrap is a hybrid empirical resampling method; block bootstrap can preserve serial dependence better than iid resampling.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0466은 scenario vs sensitivity를, N0463~N0464는 bootstrap/Monte Carlo/historical simulation을 정리했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: iid bootstrap preserves serial correlation automatically

formulas: Monte Carlo: simulate from specified stochastic process
Historical: draw/replay empirical return scenarios

quiz.question: Unseen but model-consistent scenarios 생성에 더 flexible한 것은?
Historical simulation의 strength는?

losItems.label: LOS 39.g
losItems.title: Simulation inputs, design decisions and interpretation
losItems.losText: Simulation에서 필요한 inputs/decisions를 설명하고 simulated output을 해석해.
losItems.why: Random number를 돌리는 것보다 process specification과 dependence가 핵심이야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Simulation에서 필요한 inputs/decisions를 설명하고 simulated output을 해석해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: Inputs include initial state, expected returns/drifts, volatilities, correlations/copulas, distribution tails, mean reversion, autocorrelation, horizon, rebalancing, transaction costs, behavior rules.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Interpret distributions of terminal wealth, drawdown, shortfall probability, path-dependent outcomes. Monte Carlo sampling error decreases with more paths, but parameter/model uncertainty does not.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0466~N0467은 scenario/sensitivity와 simulation inputs, N0463~N0464는 path generation을 정리했어.

sections.title: 6. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Average terminal wealth만 보고 path risk 무시

formulas: Monte Carlo estimate ≈ average/quantile across simulated path outcomes
Sampling error typically falls with more simulations, model error does not

quiz.question: Simulation path 수를 늘려도 해결되지 않는 것은?
Retirement simulation에서 mean terminal wealth만 보면 놓치는 것은?
quiz.explanation: Investor objective는 downside/path dependent일 수 있어.

losItems.label: LOS 39.h
losItems.title: Using sensitivity analysis
losItems.losText: Key inputs를 변화시켜 strategy/simulation 결과 민감도를 평가하는 sensitivity analysis를 수행·해석해.
losItems.why: Base-case 숫자가 assumptions에 얼마나 취약한지 확인하는 마지막 LOS야.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Key inputs를 변화시켜 strategy/simulation 결과 민감도를 평가하는 sensitivity analysis를 수행·해석해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition
sections.body: One-way sensitivity changes one input while holding others; multi-way changes combinations. Tornado charts rank impact. Useful inputs include transaction cost, volatility, correlation, signal decay, rebalance frequency, expected return.

sections.title: 3. Technical explanation
sections.body: Sensitivity is not a probability forecast. It identifies fragility and nonlinear thresholds. Highly sensitive strategies may require conservative assumptions or redesign.

sections.title: 5. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0468~N0469의 bias/model-risk notes와 N0466의 sensitivity-vs-scenario comparison을 통합했어.

formulas: Sensitivity output = f(input perturbation); no probability is implied unless separately modeled

quiz.question: Transaction cost assumption을 5/10/20 bp로 바꿔 net return을 비교하는 것은?
quiz.explanation: Input perturbation에 대한 output response를 봐.

quiz.question: Sensitivity analysis의 limitation은?

readings.title: Code of Ethics and Standards of Professional Conduct
readings.sourceNote: 2026 Schweser R40 backbone + Marie note ledger N0481~N0483 integrated and independently rewritten for study use.
readings.overview: Code of Ethics 6개 원칙과 Standards I–VII를 Ethics 전체의 navigation map으로 정리하고, member/candidate의 실제 ethical responsibilities로 번역한다.
readings.examPriority: 높음. R41/R42 적용의 기초 지도이며 Code vs Standards, more-strict requirement, process vs outcome 구분이 핵심이다.

losItems.label: LOS 40.a
losItems.title: Six components of the Code of Ethics and seven Standards of Professional Conduct
losItems.losText: Describe the six components of the Code of Ethics and the seven Standards of Professional Conduct.
losItems.why: Ethics 전체의 지도야. 여섯 Code principles와 Standards I–VII의 역할을 정확히 구분해야 R41 case에서 어느 duty가 걸리는지 빠르게 찾을 수 있어.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: Code of Ethics의 6개 상위 원칙과 Standards of Professional Conduct의 7개 대분류를 설명하라는 뜻이야. 단순 암기보다 Code는 ‘왜 그렇게 행동해야 하는가’, Standards는 ‘구체적으로 무엇을 해야/하지 말아야 하는가’라는 위계로 이해하면 좋아.

sections.title: 2. Intuition: Code는 나침반, Standards는 행동 규칙
sections.body: Code는 integrity, client interests, independent judgment, ethical practice, market integrity, professional competence라는 넓은 방향을 제시해. Standards는 그 방향을 실제 업무에서 적용하도록 Professionalism, Capital Markets, Clients, Employers, Research, Conflicts, CFA responsibilities로 쪼개.

sections.title: 3. Six Code components
table: 본인뿐 아니라 주변의 윤리적 행동 촉진
Promote integrity and viability of capital markets
복잡한 상품을 이해하지 못한 채 추천

sections.title: 4. Seven Standards map
table: Loyalty, Fair Dealing, Suitability, Performance, Confidentiality
V Investment Analysis, Recommendations, and Actions

sections.title: 5. 어떻게 문제에서 쓰는가
bullets: Case facts에서 먼저 actor와 duty recipient를 표시
행동의 핵심 동사—trade, recommend, disclose, solicit, accept gift, supervise—를 찾기
가장 직접적인 Standard를 우선 매핑한 뒤 다른 Standards가 동시에 걸리는지 확인
Code principle은 해석 방향을 보완하지만, violation 판단은 구체적 Standard facts에 맞춰 하기

sections.title: 6. 마리 노트 통합
sections.body: N0481은 Code → Standards → Application cases의 계층을, N0482는 여섯 Code principles를, N0483은 Standards I–VII master list를 정리했어. 사이트에서는 이 3장을 Ethics 전체 navigation backbone으로 통합했어.

sections.title: 7. Common mistakes & CFA traps
bullets: Code와 Standards를 같은 수준의 목록으로 외워 hierarchy를 잃는 것
‘고객이익 우선’만으로 모든 문제를 해결하려고 하는 것—employer, market, law duties도 존재
Ethics에는 공식이 없으니 감으로 푼다고 생각하는 것—사실은 actor→duty→fact→rule의 구조 문제
좋은 결과가 나왔으니 절차 위반도 괜찮다고 생각하는 것

sections.title: 8. 30초 회상법
sections.body: I 법·프로 / II 시장 / III 고객 / IV 고용주 / V 분석 / VI 충돌 / VII CFA. 이 7칸을 먼저 떠올리고, Code 6개는 ‘Integrity–Client–Judgment–Ethics–Market–Competence’로 묶어.

formulas: Ethics map: Code principles → Standards I–VII → case facts → required action
Memory cue: I Professionalism / II Markets / III Clients / IV Employers / V Analysis / VI Conflicts / VII CFA

quiz.question: 다음 중 Code of Ethics와 Standards의 관계를 가장 정확히 설명한 것은?
choices: Code는 구체적 금지행위만, Standards는 추상 원칙만 다룬다
Code는 상위 윤리원칙, Standards는 구체적 행동규칙을 제공한다
quiz.explanation: Code는 넓은 방향, Standards는 실제 conduct를 구체화해.

quiz.question: MNPI와 market manipulation은 주로 어느 Standard 대분류인가?
Suitability는 어느 대분류에 속하나?
quiz.explanation: Suitability는 고객의 상황·mandate·portfolio context를 다루는 III(C) 영역이야.

losItems.label: LOS 40.b
losItems.title: Ethical responsibilities required of CFA Institute members and candidates
losItems.losText: Explain the ethical responsibilities required of CFA Institute members and candidates in the CFA Program by the Code and Standards.
losItems.why: 목록을 외운 뒤 실제 책임을 설명하는 LOS야. 법보다 더 엄격한 기준, client/profession priority, independent judgment, competence와 market integrity를 실제 행동으로 번역해야 해.

sections.title: 1. 한국어로 풀면
sections.body: CFA Institute member와 candidate가 Code and Standards 아래에서 어떤 책임을 지는지 설명하라는 뜻이야. ‘무엇을 암기했는가’보다 ‘어떤 행동을 해야 하는가’로 바꿔 말할 수 있어야 해.

sections.title: 2. Intuition: Ethics는 결과가 아니라 process와 duty
sections.body: 투자성과가 좋아도 MNPI로 거래했거나, 추천 근거가 부족했거나, conflict를 숨겼다면 violation일 수 있어. 반대로 손실이 났다고 자동 violation은 아니야. Reasonable process와 duty 이행이 기준이야.

sections.title: 3. Core responsibility set
bullets: Applicable law와 Code/Standards를 이해하고 충돌 시 더 엄격한 요구를 따르기
개인 이익보다 profession integrity와 client interests를 우선하기
Independent professional judgment를 유지하고 gifts·pressure·conflicts를 통제하기
Market integrity를 훼손하는 MNPI trading과 manipulation을 피하기
Conflicts를 prominent하게 disclose하고 필요한 경우 consent 받기
CFA Program과 designation을 정확하게 표현하고 exam/program integrity를 보호하기

sections.title: 4. ‘More strict’ rule을 적용하는 사고
sections.body: Law, regulation, employer policy, Code/Standards가 다를 때 무조건 CFA rule만 따르는 게 아니야. 적용 가능한 요구 중 더 엄격한 기준을 선택하는 것이 기본 사고야. 다만 실제 jurisdiction과 legal advice는 compliance/legal function과 확인해야 해.

sections.title: 5. Responsibility hierarchy를 오해하지 말자
table: Do not trade/cause others to trade

sections.title: 6. 마리 노트 통합
